title: Kung Pao Chicken

seo.title: How to Make Kung Pao Chicken — Easy Chinese Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Learn how to make authentic Kung Pao Chicken. This easy Chinese recipe includes step-by-step instructions, tips, and serving ideas.
longTailKeywords: how to make Kung Pao Chicken
h2Headings: Pro Tips for the Best Results
seo.imageAlt: Authentic Chinese Kung Pao Chicken recipe
seo.imageCaption: Delicious homemade Kung Pao Chicken — a beloved Chinese classic

description: Kung Pao Chicken is a vibrant, spicy, and savory stir-fry dish featuring tender chicken, crunchy peanuts, and a tantalizing sauce. This Sichuan classic balances heat with a touch of sweetness and tang, creating an unforgettable flavor experience perfect for a satisfying lunch. It is a true testament to the rich culinary traditions of China, loved by many around the world.

ingredients.notes: white and light green parts sliced, green tops reserved for garnish
for sauce, mixed with 2 tbsp water

instructions.title: Marinate the Chicken
instructions.text: In a bowl, combine the cubed chicken with 2 tablespoons soy sauce, 1 tablespoon Shaoxing wine, 1 tablespoon cornstarch, and 1 teaspoon sesame oil. Mix well and let it marinate for at least 15 minutes while you prepare the other ingredients.

instructions.title: Prepare the Sauce
instructions.text: In a small bowl, whisk together 2 tablespoons soy sauce, 1 tablespoon black vinegar, 1 tablespoon sugar, and 1 tablespoon Shaoxing wine. In a separate tiny bowl, mix 1 teaspoon cornstarch with 2 tablespoons water to create a slurry. Set both aside.

instructions.title: Stir-fry Aromatics and Chilies
instructions.text: Heat 2 tablespoons of vegetable oil in a wok or large skillet over high heat until shimmering. Add the dried red chilies and Sichuan peppercorns. Stir-fry for 15-30 seconds until fragrant and the chilies slightly darken. Be careful not to burn them. Remove the chilies and peppercorns with a slotted spoon and set aside.

instructions.title: Cook the Chicken
instructions.text: Add the remaining 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil to the wok. Add the marinated chicken in a single layer and stir-fry for 3-4 minutes until browned and mostly cooked through. Remove the chicken from the wok and set aside with the chilies and peppercorns.

instructions.title: Combine Aromatics and Sauce
instructions.text: Lower the heat to medium. Add the minced garlic, ginger, and the white and light green parts of the green onions to the wok. Stir-fry for about 1 minute until fragrant. Pour in the prepared sauce mixture and bring it to a simmer. Stir in the cornstarch slurry and cook, stirring constantly, until the sauce thickens.

instructions.title: Final Assembly
instructions.text: Return the cooked chicken, chilies, and Sichuan peppercorns to the wok. Add the roasted peanuts. Toss everything together vigorously to coat the chicken evenly with the sauce. Cook for another 1-2 minutes until everything is well combined and heated through. Garnish with the reserved green onion tops before serving.

tips: To achieve tender chicken, velvet it by marinating with cornstarch and a touch of oil. This technique locks in moisture during high-heat cooking.
Adjust the number of dried chilies and Sichuan peppercorns to your preferred spice level. For more heat, leave the chilies whole; for less, remove the seeds.

Serve Kung Pao Chicken immediately over steamed white rice to best enjoy its fresh, vibrant flavors and textures. Leftovers can be stored in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days.
article: Kung Pao Chicken is a beloved Chinese Lunch Recipes dish enjoyed across Chinese. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
